text.p: I just read your profile on the net and decided to make this contact believing that you could be of immense assistance to me concerning where to do my postdoctoral research.

Am Dr. from ,Nigeria. Am a linguist (Ph.D in Linguistics from ) working on Igala language in which I have written quite extensively. Am currently a Senior Lecturer and Acting Head of Department, Languages and Linguistics, University -Nigeria. Am wondering if there will be opportunity for me to do my Post-Doctoral research in your university or any other of your recommendation. Apart from my specialization in Phonetics and Phonology, am equally interested in investigating the place of indigenous languages in indigenous knowledge development in Africa. It would be highly appreciated if this request is granted. Pls, find my CV attached.Thank you.

Igala is a dominant language spoken in Kogi State. It is spoken by over two million people who live on the eastern part of Kogi State (Kogi East Senatorial District) covering nine(9) Local Government Areas of the state: Ankpa, Bassa, Dekina, Ibaji, Idah, Igalamela/Odolu, Ofu, Olamaboro and Omala. At present, the language is spoken beyond the political boundaries of the former Igala division. Igala language belongs to the New Benue Congo group of languages ( Kwa-Sub group) and precisely one of the ‘Yoruboid’ languages. Other languages in this group are Yoruba and Itsekiri in Nigeria.
I hope to hearing from you.
